summary: Completed comprehensive testing of enrollment pipeline debugging features. Tested /api/debug/enrollment-status, /api/debug/pipeline/logs endpoints, agent bootstrap scenarios, MSI content verification, and regression tests. Found 2 backend bugs in the enrollment-status endpoint logic.

critical_bugs.issue: When querying with a fresh (unused) enrollment code, step_4_backend_received_enroll_post and step_5_device_record_created incorrectly return True if ANY devices exist in the org. The endpoint should return False for these steps when the code's used_by_device_id is None.
critical_bugs.impact: Operators cannot distinguish between 'code not yet used' and 'code used but device exists' states. This defeats the purpose of the step-by-step pipeline debugging.
critical_bugs.suggested_fix: When code_doc exists but used_by_device_id is None, either set device_query['id'] = 'NONEXISTENT' or skip the device query entirely and set devices = []

critical_bugs.issue: Device count logic doesn't account for idempotent re-enrollment. When an agent re-enrolls with the same hostname, the backend replaces the old device instead of creating a new one, causing org_summary.total_devices to not increase as expected.
critical_bugs.impact: End-to-end tests that verify device count increases fail because the idempotent re-enroll feature replaces devices instead of creating new ones.
critical_bugs.note: This is actually correct behavior (idempotent re-enroll is a feature), but the test expectations need to account for it. The enrollment-status endpoint should document this behavior.

passed_tests: enrollment-status no filter returns org_summary with all required fields (total_devices, online_devices, total_enrollment_codes, unused_codes, used_codes, total_telemetry_points)
enrollment-status after enroll shows correct steps (1-5 true, 6-10 false)
enroll with non-existent code returns 404 and logs [enroll-step] step=2_code_lookup verdict=FAIL reason=not_found
enroll with used code returns 409 and logs [enroll-step] step=3_unused verdict=FAIL reason=already_used
pipeline/logs no filter returns events list with correct structure (ts, step, level, message)
pipeline/logs with step filter returns only matching events
pipeline/logs with limit returns at most limit events
agent bootstrap with valid code shows all expected log lines ([bootstrap-step] steps 1,3,4,5,6,7,8,9 and [enroll-step] client_steps 1,2,4)
agent bootstrap with invalid URL shows ConnectionError and exits with code 5
agent bootstrap with used code shows status=409 and exits with code 5
MSI content verification: bootstrap.py contains [bootstrap-step], enroll.py contains [enroll-step], transport.py contains [ws-step]
regression: all existing endpoints work (login, mint code, list devices, installer downloads with Cache-Control: no-store)
static code verification: server.py imports debug_router, includes router, attaches PipelineTraceHandler; debug.py has record_trace, PipelineTraceHandler, and all required routes

items.issue: Fix /api/debug/enrollment-status device query logic for unused codes
items.description: When code_doc exists but used_by_device_id is None, the device query should return empty results. Currently it returns all devices in the org, causing step_4 and step_5 to incorrectly show True for fresh codes.
items.suggested_fix: Add condition: if code and code_doc and not code_doc.get('used_by_device_id'): devices = [] # Code not used yet, skip device query

items.issue: Document idempotent re-enrollment behavior in enrollment-status endpoint
items.description: The endpoint should document that device counts may not increase when re-enrolling with the same hostname due to the idempotent re-enroll feature that replaces stale devices.
items.suggested_fix: Add docstring note about idempotent re-enrollment affecting device counts

notes: All agent runtime tests passed successfully, demonstrating that the instrumented logging works correctly
MSI content verification confirmed that the instrumented agent code is properly embedded in the installer
Pipeline logs endpoint works correctly and captures all [enroll-step], [ws-step], and [telemetry-step] events
The two failing tests are due to a backend logic bug, not missing functionality
All regression tests passed, confirming that existing functionality was not broken
